description: Default context recipe for AIWizard macOS-app generation. Loads in priority order: framework scaffold > macOS app builder capability pack > active memory > universal wisdom > framework-specific wisdom (pinned to macos-app pack) > native macOS Lego components > component-reuse-hints > feature manifest > user goal > recent history. Each layer skips cleanly when its prerequisite isn't available. v2 added active-memory layer (Phase MA.3). v3 adds excludeTitlePatterns to drop CCAA / AgentRuntime / ScheduledJob log spam from retrieval results. v4 (2026-04-20): Phase DF added frontend-skill-guidance layer at priority 82. v5 (Phase CAP): added macos-app-builder capability pack at priority 98. v6 (2026-05-02): added macos-components layer at priority 84 for source-copy SwiftUI Lego components. v7 (2026-05-02): macos-components now renders contracts/assembly only; the Harness attaches canonical source-copy files after a valid marker. v8 (2026-05-04): Phase QS.3 added component-reuse-hints layer at priority 83 so the LLM sees per-component HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW reuse verdicts when picking Legos. v9 (2026-05-17): skip frontend-skill-guidance for CLI backends on native macOS app runs. v10 (2026-05-26): pin wisdom-framework to packID=macos-app (was relying on HarnessGoal.category resolver which is nil on this path, silently falling back to universal); remove frontend-skill-guidance entirely (web/landing taste pack has no business shaping SwiftUI generation, and the v9 skipForBackends guard was not being honored at assembly time). Recovers ~14KB context per call and unlocks the real 172KB macos-app wisdom pack.
